name: Red Lentil Spinach Soup
instructions: Rinse lentils and chop onion and garlic.
Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic for 2-3 minutes.
Add lentils, tomatoes, spices, and broth; simmer 20 minutes until lentils are soft.
Stir in spinach, season, and log final grams.
app_notes: Iron-rich plant-protein soup ideal for early cycle phases. Turmeric provides anti-inflammatory support.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase or reduce lentils by 20-40g to adjust protein and carbs together.
Increase or reduce olive oil by 3-8g to change fats.
Add 50g cooked chickpeas to boost protein by ~5g.

name: Chicken Vegetable Soup
instructions: Dice chicken and chop all vegetables into bite-sized pieces.
Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic for 2 minutes.
Add chicken and cook through, then add vegetables, broth, and herbs.
Simmer 20 minutes, season, and log final grams.
app_notes: High-protein lean soup perfect for follicular phase energy. Light on fat, easy to digest.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase or reduce chicken breast by 50-80g to adjust protein.
Swap or remove potato to lower carbs by ~15g.
Increase olive oil by 5-10g to add moderate fat.

name: Miso Salmon Noodle Soup
instructions: Cook soba noodles per packet, drain and set aside.
Dissolve miso paste in warm broth; poach salmon in the broth 8-10 minutes.
Add mushrooms, spinach, soy sauce, and ginger; simmer 3 minutes.
Plate noodles, ladle broth and salmon over, garnish, and log final grams.
app_notes: Omega-3 rich soup for luteal phase mood and hormone support. Miso provides gut-friendly probiotics.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase or reduce salmon by 30-50g to adjust protein and fat together.
Swap soba noodles for rice noodles to lower protein or adjust carbs.
Add or remove sesame oil by 3-5g to fine-tune fats.

name: Chickpea & Kale Soup
instructions: Chop kale and dice onion and garlic.
Heat olive oil and sauté onion and garlic for 2-3 minutes.
Add chickpeas, tomatoes, kale, cumin, and broth; simmer 15 minutes.
Finish with lemon juice, season, and log final grams.
app_notes: Iron and fibre powerhouse for menstrual phase support. Lemon juice boosts iron absorption.

macro_adjustment_rules: Add or reduce chickpeas by 40-60g to adjust protein and carbs together.
Increase olive oil by 5g to add approximately 5g fat.
Swap kale for spinach for a lighter digestion variant.

name: Beef & Barley Soup
instructions: Brown beef chunks in olive oil for 3-4 minutes; set aside.
Sauté onion and garlic, then add tomato paste and cook 1 minute.
Return beef, add barley, vegetables, broth, and thyme; simmer 30 minutes.
Season, serve hot, and log final grams.
app_notes: Hearty iron and protein-dense soup for menstrual or heavy luteal days. Batch well — tastes better next day.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase or reduce beef by 30-60g to adjust protein and fat.
Swap barley for quinoa or remove to reduce carbs by ~20g.
Reduce olive oil to 4g to lower fat by approximately 4g.

name: Thai Coconut Chicken Soup
instructions: Soak rice noodles in hot water per packet instructions, drain.
Simmer broth with lemongrass and ginger for 5 minutes.
Add chicken, coconut milk, mushrooms, bok choy, and fish sauce; cook 10 minutes.
Serve over noodles, finish with lime juice, and log final grams.
app_notes: Hormone-supportive MCT fats from coconut milk. Lemongrass and ginger add anti-inflammatory benefits.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase chicken by 40-60g to boost protein by ~10g.
Reduce or remove rice noodles to lower carbs by ~35g.
Adjust coconut milk by 20-30ml to change fat by ~5g.

name: Pumpkin Turkey Soup
instructions: Brown ground turkey in olive oil, breaking it apart, for 5 minutes.
Add onion and garlic and cook 2 minutes.
Stir in pumpkin puree, coconut milk, broth, sage, and nutmeg; simmer 15 minutes.
Blend partially if desired, season, and log final grams.
app_notes: Comforting luteal-phase soup. Pumpkin provides beta-carotene; turkey supports progesterone balance.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase ground turkey by 30-50g to boost protein by ~8g.
Add or reduce pumpkin puree by 50g to adjust carbs by ~5g.
Swap coconut milk for broth to lower fat by ~5g.

name: White Bean & Tuna Soup
instructions: Drain tuna and rinse white beans.
Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil for 2 minutes.
Add beans, tomatoes, broth, and tuna; simmer 10 minutes.
Stir in spinach, finish with lemon juice, and log final grams.
app_notes: All-phase high-protein soup that's fast and macro-friendly. White beans provide resistant starch and iron.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase tuna by 30-50g to boost protein by ~8g.
Add or reduce white beans by 50g to adjust carbs and protein.
Increase olive oil by 5g to add approximately 5g fat.

name: Greek Yogurt Protein Bowl
instructions: Stir protein powder into Greek yogurt until smooth.
Slice banana and measure berries.
Layer yogurt base with fruit and granola.
Drizzle honey, add chia seeds, and log final grams.
app_notes: Quick high-protein breakfast ideal for follicular energy surge. Berries support oestrogen metabolism.

macro_adjustment_rules: Add 10g protein powder to boost protein by ~8g.
Reduce granola by 15g to lower carbs by ~10g.
Swap full-fat yogurt for low-fat to reduce fat by ~5g.

name: Smoked Salmon Scramble
instructions: Whisk eggs and season with black pepper.
Heat olive oil over medium-low heat and cook eggs slowly, stirring gently.
Fold in cream cheese, smoked salmon, and spinach in the last minute.
Plate with capers and red onion; log final grams.
app_notes: Omega-3 and choline-rich breakfast for early cycle phases. High fat supports hormone production.

macro_adjustment_rules: Add one egg (~50g) to increase protein by ~6g and fat by ~5g.
Increase smoked salmon by 30g to boost protein by ~7g.
Omit cream cheese to reduce fat by ~5g.

name: Protein Pancakes
instructions: Blend oats, protein powder, eggs, banana, yogurt, and baking powder into a batter.
Heat coconut oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Pour small rounds of batter and cook 2 minutes per side.
Top with berries and log final grams.
app_notes: High-carb, high-protein pre-workout breakfast for follicular and ovulatory energy peaks.

macro_adjustment_rules: Add 10g protein powder to increase protein by ~8g.
Reduce oats by 20g to lower carbs by ~13g.
Replace coconut oil with cooking spray to reduce fat by ~7g.

name: Spinach Feta Omelette
instructions: Whisk eggs and season with salt and oregano.
Heat olive oil and sauté red onion 2 minutes; add spinach and wilt.
Pour in eggs and cook until nearly set, then add feta and tomatoes to one half.
Fold omelette, plate, and log final grams.
app_notes: Versatile all-phase omelette. Feta provides calcium for PMS relief; eggs supply choline for hormone synthesis.

macro_adjustment_rules: Add one egg (~50g) to increase protein by ~6g and fat by ~5g.
Reduce feta to 20g to lower fat and sodium.
Add 30g cooked quinoa to increase carbs by ~7g.

name: Chia Berry Pudding
instructions: Mix chia seeds, almond milk, vanilla, and honey; refrigerate overnight.
In the morning, stir in Greek yogurt until combined.
Top with mixed berries and flaxseed.
Log final grams before eating.
app_notes: Prep the night before for an effortless luteal-phase breakfast. Flaxseed supports oestrogen metabolism.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase Greek yogurt by 50g to boost protein by ~5g.
Reduce honey to 5g or omit to lower carbs by ~5g.
Increase chia seeds by 5g to add approximately 2.5g fat and 1g protein.

name: Avocado Egg Toast
instructions: Toast sourdough bread slices.
Fry or poach eggs to your preference in olive oil.
Mash avocado with lemon juice, salt, and chilli flakes.
Spread avocado on toast, top with eggs and tomatoes, and log final grams.
app_notes: Classic nutrient-dense breakfast. Avocado provides monounsaturated fats that support oestrogen phase hormones.

macro_adjustment_rules: Add one egg to increase protein by ~6g and fat by ~5g.
Add one more slice of bread (~45g) to increase carbs by ~20g.
Reduce avocado by 30g to lower fat by ~5g.

name: Quinoa Berry Breakfast Bowl
instructions: Warm cooked quinoa with almond milk and cinnamon for 2-3 minutes.
Stir in honey and almond butter until combined.
Top with mixed berries and pumpkin seeds.
Log final grams before eating.
app_notes: Magnesium-rich breakfast for menstrual and luteal phases. Pumpkin seeds and quinoa support iron and zinc intake.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase quinoa by 50g to add approximately 4g protein and 14g carbs.
Reduce or remove honey to lower carbs by ~8g.
Increase almond butter by 10g to add approximately 5g fat.

name: Cottage Cheese Fruit Bowl
instructions: Scoop cottage cheese into a bowl.
Add pineapple, berries, and walnuts.
Drizzle with honey and sprinkle flaxseed.
Log final grams before eating.
app_notes: All-phase light and easy high-protein breakfast. Cottage cheese is rich in casein, which supports overnight muscle repair.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase cottage cheese by 50g to boost protein by ~8g.
Reduce pineapple by 30g to lower carbs by ~6g.
Add or reduce walnuts by 10g to change fat by ~6g.

name: Apple & Almond Butter
instructions: Slice apple into wedges.
Portion almond butter into a small bowl.
Dust with cinnamon.
Log final grams before eating.
app_notes: Classic luteal-phase snack. Almond butter provides magnesium and healthy fats to ease PMS cravings.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase almond butter by 8g to add ~3g protein and ~5g fat.
Reduce apple to 100g to lower carbs by ~8g.
Weigh ingredients carefully — this snack is calorie-dense per gram.

name: Hummus & Veggie Plate
instructions: Wash and cut vegetables into sticks and halves.
Portion hummus into a small bowl.
Arrange vegetables around hummus.
Log final grams before eating.
app_notes: Light, fibre-rich snack ideal for follicular phase when appetite is naturally lower.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase hummus by 30g to add ~3g protein, ~3g carbs, and ~3g fat.
Add or swap vegetables freely — most non-starchy vegetables are negligible in calories.
Weigh hummus carefully — fat and calories add up quickly.

name: Greek Yogurt Nut Mix
instructions: Spoon Greek yogurt into a small bowl.
Top with mixed nuts and drizzle honey.
Dust with cinnamon.
Log final grams before eating.
app_notes: All-phase high-protein snack. Greek yogurt feeds gut bacteria and mixed nuts deliver hormone-supporting fats.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase Greek yogurt by 50g to boost protein by ~5g.
Remove honey to reduce carbs by ~7g.
Increase mixed nuts by 10g to add approximately 6g fat and 2g protein.

name: Dark Chocolate Walnut Bite
instructions: Break chocolate into squares and measure walnuts.
Combine on a small plate with dried cherries.
Log exact grams before eating.
Eat mindfully and note how it affects your cravings.
app_notes: Dark chocolate is rich in magnesium and antioxidants that directly support PMS symptom relief. Satisfies cravings without sugar spike.

macro_adjustment_rules: Reduce dark chocolate to 20g to lower fat by ~5g and carbs by ~3g.
Remove dried cherries to reduce carbs by ~7g.
Weigh all ingredients precisely — this is a calorie-dense snack.

name: Edamame with Sea Salt
instructions: Cook edamame from frozen in boiling water for 4-5 minutes or microwave 3 minutes.
Drain and pat dry.
Season with sea salt and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Log final grams of edamame beans only (discard pods).
app_notes: Edamame is one of the best plant-based protein snacks. Phytoestrogens support follicular and ovulatory phase oestrogen activity.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase portion by 50g pods to add ~4g protein, ~4g carbs, ~2g fat.
Season with soy sauce for an umami variant without changing macros significantly.
Note: log only the bean weight, not the pod weight.

name: Rice Cake Salmon Bites
instructions: Spread cream cheese onto rice cakes.
Layer smoked salmon and cucumber slices on top.
Add capers and a squeeze of lemon juice.
Log final grams before eating.
app_notes: Light omega-3 rich snack for menstrual phase. Smoked salmon provides haem iron and DHA for mood support.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase smoked salmon by 20-30g to boost protein by ~5g.
Add one more rice cake (~10g) to increase carbs by ~8g.
Swap light cream cheese for full-fat to add ~3g fat per 20g.

name: Caesar Chicken Salad
instructions: Grill or pan-cook chicken breast and slice.
Wash and roughly chop romaine lettuce.
Toss lettuce with dressing, parmesan, and lemon juice.
Top with chicken and croutons; log final grams.
app_notes: Classic high-protein salad that hits macro targets efficiently. Opt for light dressing to reduce fat if needed.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase chicken by 40-60g to boost protein by ~9-14g.
Remove croutons to cut carbs by ~10g.
Reduce dressing to 15g to lower fat by ~7g.

name: Salmon Avocado Salad
instructions: Pan-cook or bake salmon fillet and let cool slightly.
Slice avocado and prep vegetables.
Whisk olive oil and lemon juice for dressing.
Toss greens and vegetables with dressing, top with salmon and avocado, and log final grams.
app_notes: The most hormone-supportive salad in the database. Omega-3 and monounsaturated fats from both salmon and avocado.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase salmon by 30-50g to boost protein by ~7-11g.
Reduce avocado to 50g to lower fat by ~7g.
Add 50g cooked quinoa to increase carbs by ~12g and protein by ~2g.

name: Greek Chicken Salad
instructions: Grill chicken breast with oregano and slice.
Chop all vegetables and halve tomatoes.
Whisk olive oil and vinegar into a dressing.
Combine all ingredients, crumble feta on top, and log final grams.
app_notes: Classic Greek salad elevated with grilled chicken. Feta provides calcium for muscle function and PMS support.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase chicken by 40-60g to boost protein by ~9-14g.
Reduce feta to 20g to lower fat and sodium.
Remove olives to reduce fat by ~4g.

name: Quinoa Roasted Veg Salad
instructions: Dice sweet potato, beetroot, red pepper, and onion; toss with half the olive oil.
Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes until caramelised.
Whisk remaining olive oil with balsamic vinegar for dressing.
Toss quinoa and spinach with roasted veg and dressing, crumble feta, add seeds, and log final grams.
app_notes: Magnesium, iron, and complex carbs for menstrual and luteal phases. Beetroot supports blood flow and energy.

macro_adjustment_rules: Add 50g cooked chickpeas to boost protein by ~5g.
Reduce sweet potato by 50g to lower carbs by ~15g.
Increase olive oil by 5g to add approximately 5g fat.

name: Tuna Niçoise Salad
instructions: Boil eggs and steam green beans until tender-crisp; cool.
Quarter baby potatoes (pre-cooked) and halve tomatoes.
Whisk olive oil, mustard, and vinegar for dressing.
Arrange all ingredients on greens, drizzle dressing, and log final grams.
app_notes: Classic French salad providing complete protein from both tuna and eggs, with iron support from greens.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase tuna by 40g to boost protein by ~10g.
Remove potatoes to reduce carbs by ~16g.
Increase or reduce olive oil by 5g to change fat by ~5g.

name: Iron-Rich Chickpea Salad
instructions: Drain and rinse chickpeas.
Wash and roughly chop spinach; prep remaining vegetables.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, cumin, and turmeric into a dressing.
Toss all ingredients together, top with pumpkin seeds, and log final grams.
app_notes: Highest plant-based iron in the salad category. Lemon juice in dressing maximises non-haem iron absorption.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase chickpeas by 50g to boost protein by ~4g and carbs by ~11g.
Remove sun-dried tomatoes to reduce carbs and fat by ~3-4g each.
Add 30g of crumbled feta to increase protein by ~4g and fat by ~5g.

name: Turkey Harvest Salad
instructions: Roast sweet potato cubes at 200°C for 20 minutes.
Cook turkey breast and slice.
Whisk olive oil, apple cider vinegar, and mustard for dressing.
Combine greens, sweet potato, cranberries, walnuts, and turkey; drizzle dressing and log final grams.
app_notes: Balanced luteal-phase salad with complex carbs for progesterone support and walnuts for omega-3 and magnesium.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase turkey by 40-60g to boost protein by ~9-13g.
Reduce sweet potato by 50g to lower carbs by ~12g.
Omit or reduce walnuts to lower fat by ~6g per 10g removed.

name: Asian Sesame Chicken Salad
instructions: Grill chicken breast with a light soy glaze and slice thin.
Shred cabbage and carrot; slice cucumber.
Whisk sesame oil, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and ginger for dressing.
Toss all salad components with dressing, top with chicken and sesame seeds, and log final grams.
app_notes: Follicular and ovulatory phase salad with phytoestrogens from edamame. Sesame seeds provide calcium and zinc.

macro_adjustment_rules: Increase chicken by 40-60g to boost protein by ~9-13g.
Increase edamame by 30g to add ~5g protein and ~5g carbs.
Reduce sesame oil by 4g to lower fat by approximately 4g.
